summary: Tests cover core parsing (XML/JSON), aggregation, and flaky detection through both unit tests and workflow integration tests. However, markdown output format is never validated—only substring matching of aggregate counts. CLI argument handling (--output, --no-fail), return codes, and GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY file writing are untested. Workflow tests use only 2 fixture scenarios, missing edge cases (empty suite, all-skipped, single test). Unit tests lack boundary conditions (0 total, pass rate edge cases) and XML format variations (single <testsuite> root, missing classname, <error> elements). Fixture files referenced in test_workflow.py (run1.xml, run2.json) are read from disk but not shown/documented, creating brittleness and unclear test setup. Design is clean with good pytest usage and helper functions, but assertions are shallow (aggregate string presence rather than markdown structure validation). Would catch major parsing regressions but miss markdown, CLI, and edge-case failures.
